name: The Castles of the Valley Trail
name_original: Ayora a Zarra a Cofrentes a Jalance a Jarafuel — circular por Ruta de los Castillos
description: This difficult 38.9-mile mountain biking route in Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes explores castles and diverse terrain, requiring good fitness.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ayora offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes in the inland Valencian Community. The region features a network of forest tracks, trails, and old roads that traverse valleys, mountains, and canyons. Key features include the Ayora-Cofrentes Valley, surrounded by mountain ranges like Pico de Caroche and Sierra Palomera, and extensive pine forests such as La Hunde. This varied terrain provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails in Ayora The most popular mountain bike trail is The Castles of the Valley Trail, a 38.9 miles (62.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive riding through the region's historical and natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta de Montemayor desde Ayora, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.9 km) path. This route climbs through olive groves towards Montemayor, providing views across the Ayora valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo de Almansa desde Ayora — circular, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Ayora is defined by forest tracks, mountain ranges, and river valleys, with routes winding through pine forests and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Ayora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ayora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Ayora?
A: Ayora offers a wide selection of mountain biking routes, with over 40 trails available on komoot. These routes traverse diverse terrain, from valleys and canyons to mountain ranges and pine forests.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ayora?
A: The terrain in Ayora is diverse and rugged, featuring a network of forest tracks, trails, and old roads. You'll encounter routes winding through olive groves, steep mountain ascents, descents, and extensive pine forests like La Hunde. The region is characterized by its valleys, mountains, and canyons.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ayora?
A: Yes, Ayora has options for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 3 easy trails available, making it possible to find suitable options for less experienced riders or families looking for a gentler ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1335036985/vuelta-de-montemayor-desde-ayora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta de Montemayor desde Ayora&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that climbs through olive groves and offers scenic views.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Ayora?
A: Absolutely. Ayora is known for its challenging terrain, with 3 difficult routes available. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and technical sections, requiring good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1337913969/the-castles-of-the-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castles of the Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 63 km long with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Ayora?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Ayora are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336203501/castillo-de-almansa-desde-ayora-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almansa desde Ayora — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 57 km through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Ayora's trails offer numerous scenic spots. You can ride towards peaks like Pico de Caroche or Montemayor for breathtaking panoramic views. The region also features natural areas like La Hunde, known for its vast pine forests. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4010315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cinto de la Cabra&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Caroche&lt;/a&gt; itself offers extensive vistas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ayora?
A: The mountain biking routes in Ayora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the stunning natural landscapes that make for a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any specific natural reserves or forests to explore by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region is home to significant natural areas. La Hunde is a notable natural reserve with a vast pine forest, offering dedicated mountain biking routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328874000/la-hunde-pico-palomera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hunde - Pico Palomera Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This area is recognized for its ecological value and provides an immersive forest riding experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: While riding, you can encounter historical elements such as the Ayora Castle, which offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1337913969/the-castles-of-the-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castles of the Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are designed to take you through the region's historical and natural landscapes, hinting at the area's rich past.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ayora?
A: Ayora's inland location in the Valencian Community generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, while winters are milder but can still have cooler days, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that feature red rock formations or unique geological features?
A: Yes, you can find routes that highlight unique geological features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recreation area – Beautiful red rock face loop from Ayora&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions a beautiful red rock face, offering a distinct visual experience during your ride.
tips: It is a compendium of architecture between the 16th and 17th centuries, with a Renaissance and Mannerist style. Since July it is open for tourist visits every Saturday from 12 to 13:30.
Perfect place for a stop with benches, there is water but not for drinking.

It is a castle of Islamic origin dating from the 11th century built on one of the chimneys of the Cerro Agrás volcano and on the banks of the Cabriel river. It can be visited daily by appointment at the tourist office.
Beautiful and it makes you happy if you stop here for a while and take your time.

Jarafuel is located on the Castle Route in a beautiful wild nature. The ruins and castles have history with Templars, Romans, Moors etc. and are usually located on beautiful viewpoints.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this mountain biking route is approximately 4 hours and 51 minutes. This is an average time and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit points of interest.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Castillos mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is officially rated as difficult and requires a very good physical condition. While some sections might be manageable, the overall route is demanding due to its length and elevation changes. It's not recommended for beginners looking for an easy ride, but experienced riders will find it a rewarding challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter approximately 28.5 km of paved sections, about 11.8 km of asphalt, and around 10.3 km of unpaved tracks. There are also natural paths, single tracks, and various types of roads including national roads, streets, and access roads, offering a varied riding experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Ruta de los Castillos mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: As its name suggests, the route is rich in historical castles and offers stunning natural beauty. You'll pass by the impressive Cofrentes Castle. Other notable points include the Parish Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Ayora, and several fountains like La Musica Fountain, Fountain of the Four Spouts, and Fuente del Jarral. The trail winds through the picturesque Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes, characterized by valleys, diverse flora and fauna, and rugged landscapes with pine and juniper forests.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Valencia, Spain, the best times to ride this trail are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for demanding physical activity, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, starting and ending in Ayora. You can typically find parking within the town of Ayora to begin your ride. Specific trailhead access points might vary, but starting from the town center or a designated parking area in Ayora is a common approach for circular routes like this.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de los Castillos mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not provided, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas in Spain, especially if kept on a leash. However, given the length and difficulty of this mountain biking route, it might be very challenging for most dogs. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain, and carry plenty of water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes or this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes region or to ride this particular mountain biking trail. It is generally open to the public for recreational use. However, always respect local regulations and private property along the route.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. A significant portion (53%) follows the 'Los Castillos del Valle' trail. You will also ride along parts of 'La Ruta de Jalance' (11%), 'El Campichuelo' (8%), 'Camino del Balneario' (6%), 'Jarafuel, Muela de Juey y el Castillico' (6%), and 'Los Cañones del Júcar' (6%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or rest areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a couple of recreational areas that could serve as rest stops. You'll find the Tollo Pillete Recreational and Camping Area and the Fuente del Tobarro Recreation Area. These spots often provide picnic tables or natural spaces to take a break. Additionally, passing through towns like Ayora, Zarra, Cofrentes, Jalance, and Jarafuel offers opportunities for cafes, shops, and other amenities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, it's crucial to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water and snacks, as resupply points might be spaced out. Essential mountain biking gear includes a helmet, gloves, repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and appropriate clothing for the weather. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the komoot app is highly recommended for navigation, as some sections might be less clearly marked.
